ps140: O most glorious wonder!* The Maker of all has assumed flesh,* wishing to become a man in His loving compassion,* showing the Hebrews a wealth of miracles.* Wherefore at Bethesda’s pool,* He wrought healing upon the paralytic man, saying to him:* Take up your bed and walk.

O Saviour, my Lord and God,* since You willed to restore fallen mankind,* as a man You wandered the earth curing all manner of infirmities,* for the sake of the abundance of Your loving compassion.* Thus having come to the porch of the Sheep’s Pool,* You, by Your word, raised up the paralytic,* who had lain prostrate for thirty and eight years.

Utterly consumed with jealousy, the Hebrews brought guilt upon themselves* making Your good works, O supremely good Lord,* an occasion and excuse for their bitter and frenzied rage.* While they themselves ceased not to transgress the Law,* they sought to slay You, who are the Life of all;* for on the Sabbath day, You showed forth as a man completely whole* him who before had been a paralytic.

A man in sickness was lying at the Sheep Pool,* and having seen You, O Lord, he cried out:* “There is no man at my side* to put me into the water when it is stirred.* When I arrive, another comes before me and receives the healing,* and I lie in sickness.”* And immediately the Saviour, having mercy on him, says to him:* “For your sake I became man;* for your sake I took on flesh.* How do you say you have no man at your side?* Take up your pallet and walk!”* Everything is possible for You;* everything listens to You;* everything obeys You.* Remember us, O Holy One,* and have mercy on us, as You love humankind.
aposticha: O ineffable goodness who created the human form,* You came to heal those who are ill.* O Christ, by Your word You raised the Paralytic at the Sheep Pool;* You healed the suffering of the woman with the flow of blood;* You showed mercy to the tormented daughter of the Canaanite woman;* and You did not disdain the prayer of the centurion.* Because of this we cry out in praise:* O Almighty Lord, glory to You!
